analysis.analysis_para1: Global markets present a mixed picture this morning, with the Nikkei 225 up +0.89% and the DAX gaining +0.52%, while the FTSE 100 lags at -0.11%. In the US, major indices are experiencing a slight pullback in pre-market trading, with the S&P 500 down -0.24% at 7109.14 and the Nasdaq 100 lower by -0.31% at 26590.34. Despite this modest dip, underlying market health appears robust, as evidenced by a normal VIX at 18.79 and a stable 10Y Yield at 4.25%, indicating no immediate credit market stress. Furthermore, market breadth remains strong with 66.9% of stocks above their 50-day moving average (S5FI) and 62.9% above their 200-day moving average (MMTH), suggesting sustained participation beneath the index level.
analysis.analysis_para2: Our mechanical plan for today focuses on monitoring key support levels for the major US indices. For the S&P 500, holding above 7100 will be crucial to prevent further downside, with a potential rebound targeting 7150; a breach could see a test of 7050. Similarly, the Nasdaq 100 should ideally hold above 26500, where a bounce would signal continued tech resilience. Given the strong underlying breadth, we will prioritize identifying relative strength within leading sectors like Semiconductors, Hardware, and Telecommunications, looking for entries on dips if major index support holds and individual stocks exhibit sustained buying volume.
analysis.technical_signal: No reversal pattern confirmed yet. US indices are experiencing a minor pre-market pullback, but strong market breadth indicators suggest underlying strength is still present.

ticker_intel.reason: With an RS of 99 and a +35.6% return over the last month, AXTI is a top-tier semiconductor play poised for continued outperformance in a tech-favored market.
AAOI boasts an RS of 98 and an astounding +60.4% monthly gain, positioning it as a leading momentum stock within the robust Telecommunications sector.

Despite an RS of 97 and a +44.3% 1-month return, AMKR's 1-month gain nearly equals its 3-month performance, suggesting a recently overheated move that could be vulnerable to profit-taking in a mixed market.
